title: Expand localized HIG screenshot proof beyond launch surface
summary: Added automation/smoke/capture_localized_surfaces.py — an idb-first multisurface harness with a default catalog of 8 scoped HIG surfaces (today, root tabs ×4, build-info, empty-state-today, date-count-plural-today). Modes: --check-dependencies, --list-surfaces, --dry-run, --capture. Captures write deterministic NN-locale-<locale>-surface-<surface_id>.png files plus a manifest.json with locale, surface_id, file, bytes, sha256, navigation_steps_executed, git_commit_short, target.udid, and non_claims. Settled-state assertions support locale-specific overrides via --label-overrides; captures whose assertion fails are recorded as blocked, not silently treated as proof. 14 new unit tests cover catalog, args, plan, navigation, and guards. New Makefile target localization-multisurface-screenshot-idb-check mirrors the existing idb dependency check. Proof directory is reserved with a README documenting expected contents and non-claims; no real --capture run has landed.
not_claimed: screenshot-reviewed for any new (locale, surface) pair (no --capture run has landed)
full localized navigation works for every surface across all 19 locales
residual_risk: Settled-state assertions in the default catalog use English-plus-a-few-locales fallback labels. Locales without a matching label will block on settled-assertion-failed unless --label-overrides is supplied. This is intentional honesty, not a defect.

Empty-state-today and date-count-plural-today surfaces currently rely on natural fixture state; capturing reliable proof requires a --owlory-ui-testing launch argument or fixture seeding that is not yet implemented at the app level.
Build-info navigation taps localized 'Settings' / 'Build Info' labels; for locales where Settings is heavily translated the tap will fail until per-locale label overrides are recorded.
No actual screenshot bundle exists yet in the proof directory; downstream per-bucket HIG gate slices must run --capture and append proof_references to the all-locale HIG evidence matrix.
